config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara yang lemah atau minoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi individu sekaligus mencari kepentingan bersama, bukan mengorbankan yang satu demi yang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea makes me wary because our history shows how easily "the common good" can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or government overreach, especially against minorities. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public safety and basic public health do matter, but those limits should be narrow and justified, not a blanket principle that society always comes first. A healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only restricts them when there is a very strong, specific reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu kalau dipakai sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman dan ketidakadilan terhadap kelompok kecil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu harus jadi pagar agar negara, mayoritas, atau aparat tidak semena-mena atas nama kebaikan bersama. Saya setuju ada keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan umum atau krisis kesehatan di mana hak bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh jadi kebiasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi martabat tiap orang sambil mencari kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. In the United States, I have seen how quickly appeals to "the greater good" can turn into excuses for overreach, whether that means censorship, discrimination, or punishing unpopular minorities. Society does have legitimate needs, especially for public safety and health, but those limits on rights should be narrow, temporary, and carefully justified. If we reverse the default and put society first, I think we make it too easy for power to silence the people who need protection most.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, frasa “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menutup kritik, menekan minoritas, atau membenarkan tindakan aparat dan mayoritas yang berlebihan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu harus menjadi batas yang jelas agar kekuasaan tidak berubah jadi alat penindasan. Kalau ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana atau krisis kesehatan, pembatasan hak bisa saja perlu, tetapi harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests automatically outrank individual rights. From an American perspective, that framing worries me because it can too easily justify government overreach, majoritarian pressure, or the silencing of unpopular people under the banner of the common good. I do think there are real cases, like emergencies or public health threats, where some rights can be limited, but those limits should be narrow, temporary, and heavily scrutinized. In my view, a free society works best when individual rights are the default and collective needs are the exception, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
